description: Length-matched V2 facts for protein folding domain.

facts.context: AlphaFold2 solved static structure prediction but not:
facts.truth: the dynamics of folding pathways and kinetic intermediates
distractors: three-dimensional structure determination from amino acid sequence
evolutionary conservation patterns across homologous protein families
secondary structure prediction from primary sequence composition

facts.context: Levinthal's paradox highlights that proteins fold rapidly despite:
facts.truth: an astronomically vast conformational search space to explore
distractors: a highly restricted number of available amino acid residue types
completely identical sequences being conserved across all species
simple two-state transitions without any intermediate conformations

facts.context: Intrinsically disordered proteins challenge the folding paradigm because:
facts.truth: they perform biological functions without adopting stable 3D structure
distractors: they reach their folded state faster than typical ordered protein does
they lack any biological function and are merely degradation products
they inevitably aggregate into toxic amyloid fibrils in all conditions

facts.context: Molecular chaperones assist protein folding by:
facts.truth: preventing aggregation of partially folded intermediate states
distractors: directly specifying and dictating the final native folded structure
synthesizing complete proteins from individual amino acid building blocks
immediately degrading every misfolded protein without rescue attempts

facts.context: The folding funnel model describes protein folding as:
facts.truth: navigation of an energy landscape funneling toward the native minimum
distractors: a completely random walk through conformations with no energy bias
a single defined sequential pathway through specific intermediate states
an uphill energy climb required to reach the functional folded state

facts.truth: protein misfolding into cross-beta-sheet rich aggregate fibrils
distractors: insufficient protein production leading to cellular deficiency states
correctly folded proteins that are delivered to the wrong cell location
purely genetic mutations without any protein misfolding involved at all

facts.truth: inducing normal protein copies to adopt the misfolded conformation
distractors: replicating through a nucleic acid genome like conventional viral agents
dividing through cell fission in a manner similar to bacterial pathogens
causing random genetic mutations within the host organism DNA sequence

facts.truth: during synthesis while still being translated on the ribosome complex
distractors: exclusively after complete release and dissociation from the ribosome
only within the endoplasmic reticulum after full ribosomal translation
without any ribosome involvement in the folding process whatsoever

facts.context: Multi-domain protein folding is particularly complex because:
facts.truth: individual domains can misfold or aggregate during the synthesis process
distractors: each separate domain folds completely independently without interference
multi-domain proteins are extremely rare and do not occur in nature
only single-domain proteins are capable of misfolding into aggregates

facts.context: Membrane protein folding differs from soluble protein folding because:
facts.truth: it requires the lipid bilayer environment for proper structural formation
distractors: membrane proteins fold through identical mechanisms as soluble proteins do
membrane proteins possess no tertiary structure or three-dimensional folds
folding always finishes completely before the protein inserts into membrane

facts.context: De novo protein design validates folding understanding by:
facts.truth: creating entirely new folds and functions not found anywhere in nature
distractors: only copying and replicating existing natural protein structural folds
demonstrating that protein folding cannot ever be predicted or designed
confirming that all possible protein folds already exist in current life

facts.context: Kinetic traps in protein folding are:
facts.truth: local energy minima that delay the protein from reaching native state
distractors: identical to the native state global minimum energy conformation itself
beneficial intermediate states that accelerate correct folding kinetics
purely theoretical constructs that have never been observed in practice
